title: What I think about the Egyptian Revolution - Request

transcript.text: Hello, hello. A couple weeks ago, Time suggested that I do a video talking about the revolution in Egypt and what kind of ramifications that would create in society. He reckoned it would create a lot, and I think so, too. I think it's a pretty momentous thing that's going on.

Yeah, so basically what happened was we had the president of Egypt, Mubarak, in power for like 30 years. He's a total dictator. He wasn't being fair with the business, creating too much power and wealth discrepancy.
Then they ousted him. They had a revolution in ousted him, and then they democratically elected their first president, Morsi, and he'd been in power for like a year or something like that.
But after he got into power, he changed rules, kind of like what Hitler did, get in power democratically and then change all the rules.
I changed all the rules. I changed the Constitution, made it all more, made it a Sharia law, a Muslim law, and then wasn't doing a good job of administering, of taking care of the country.
They weren't picking up the garbage. The country was just falling apart.
And so then they had the other revolution where 15 million people were in the streets protesting, which was the largest protest ever.
And a million people were protesting for Morsi.

And after he got ousted by the army, the Muslim Brotherhood, his party, the extreme Muslim party, were saying that it was a coup and that if the rest of the world knew exactly what happened, they wouldn't stand for it because it's against the law and that they're going to fight against it.
And so now people are thinking, oh, no, no, are we going to have like a civil war now because of all the Muslims fighting?
And so, yeah, what kind of ramifications are these going to make?
Well, I think that these Muslims, man, they put in the Sharia law, you know, the law that's 1,400 years old, Islamic law in these countries.
And they think that that's the best way to do it because they live in Islamic countries and there's still a lot of, you know, super religious Muslim people in those countries.
And so they think that's the best thing, you know, because they're all religion.
They need to follow the law.
But but they these are these are old laws and it's not it's out of date and it's time for like a religious revolution, you know, and and so people aren't going to stand for it.
And I think now because of this revolution, this is like the tipping point that's actually where the masses of the people are saying, you know, no, no to the Sharia law is Muslim law.
And they're not going to stand for it.
And, you know, with them, when the Muslim Brotherhood says that they broke the law by ousting him, well, they broke the law when they changed the Constitution after he got elected.
And then and then like changing, taking, you know, power away from the secular part and bringing it to the Muslim Brotherhood.
You know, you can't overstep your bounds.
That's that's that's that's, you know, law legal reasons right there to house the president.
We've we've impeached presidents before.
We impeached presidents for a lot less than that from what he did.
So. So, yeah, I think this is the beginning.
I don't I don't. And it's funny.

See, I think this is the beginning where people in the Middle East are going to realize that that, you know, being all that Sharia law and then like fighting with violence for what you believe is not the way to go.
All these Muslims are saying, oh, yeah, well, we're going to fight.
We're going to fight even against violent because that's what Mohammed would do.
That's how Mohammed that's what he says in the Koran.
You have to fight jihad, fire with fire.
And so, you know, they'll do that and then they'll get their asses kicked.
And then and then everybody will basically, you know, start to realize that.
Like, look, we don't want to have an Islam religion in our country that's all about violence like we're sick of it.
And I think within a year or two, people are going to finally get sick of all the whole violence related to Islam and say, OK, yeah, they'll adapt it a little bit.
And they'll say, yeah, yeah, jihad means you have to fight, fight, fight with what you believe with words, with words, not with guns, with words.

And then because of that, they'll understand that it's OK to debate and then people, you know, it's OK to speak your mind and talk about, you know, expanding on all of the religious ideas and making it more modern, you know, allowing more freedom of speech.
Egypt is a country that's very old and they have a lot of pride.
And if Egypt can do it, then I think it'll spread to the rest of the Middle East, especially with all of this civil war that's been going on in Syria, that's just not ending.
You know, Syria is completely gutted now.
It's like it's like a that's it's like a waste area, waste zone.
So, you know, I think that's going to exhaust people in the region as well.
And they're finally going to throw up their hands and say, OK, we don't want any more of this.
We're done fighting. Let's just talk.
Let's just, you know, have I have, you know, more and more of a dialogue and more speaking.
Let's getting upset just because somebody disagrees with me and and.
And it'll it'll it'll get better.
I mean, that's that's that's what I think and what I hope.
But, you know, maybe maybe they still have more fight in them.
Maybe these people have so much fight left in them that that they'll just they'll just keep fighting each other and shooting each other for the next 20 or 30 years.
And I just hope that they don't, you know, none of these people get their hands on a nuclear weapon and like nuke themselves.
But I don't think that's going to happen.
I think that that within the next five or 10 years, they'll get their shit together and elect people who are more more democratic and speak more.
But but but it's exciting to see all those people just just oust the president and get a new guy.
I don't think they've they've elected a new guy in there.
But but it's it's I think this is the last straw.

This is I don't think these hardcore Muslims are going to go are going to have much more more more momentum behind them because more and more people are starting to realize how really ridiculous it all is.
And it's and it's and they're just going to be more and more the laughing stock of the world right now.

But once America, we get all of our troops back to America and we it wouldn't take it's not going to take long for people to forget about all the imperial military imperialism of America.
And I think America getting being less less militarily imperial and spending our money on renewable energy and stuff like this instead of the military.
We spend 20 percent of our money in the military.
It'll take like five years after that until the public opinion will just be, you know, not not with with America.
It'll be against the violent people in the Middle East.
And they just there's just there's going to be too many people just pissed off at them throughout the world.
And and they'll they'll finally be like, all right, when we give up, let's just let's just use the let's just go the peaceful way.
And and, you know, and call.
We'll just fight with words, fight with words, calm words and respectful and and because, you know, it's a whole world's watching and your voice can be heard.
All you have to do is upload a video and talk about it.
And I think people are going to start doing that.
People are in Europe and I mean, Egypt are going to start up on videos and expressing themselves pretty within the next, I don't know, five and ten years, five or ten years.
I think stuff's going to happen pretty fast.
I think stuff's really going to start to get changed really fast, starting in about five years.
I think from here in five years, it'll kind of people will kind of start.
But then after five years, people are going to be like, you know what, let's upload some videos.
Let's explain ourselves.
Let's let's talk.
And then you'll actually be able to have a one on two way conversation with like the equivalent of a hardcore Muslim in those days and five years.
And if not, we've got a pretty interesting show coming, they're going to give us a pretty interesting war coming down the pipeline, but I don't think that's going to happen.
Okay, peace out.
